Expected 1-Year Return
—
add a holding to see the weighted estimate
Risk (1-Year)
Methodology: Expected return = Σ wi·ri where ri is your manual override if entered, else the fund's actual 1Y return. SD / Sharpe / Max DD are naive-weighted (Σ wi·xi) — this OVER-states portfolio risk because it ignores diversification (assumes 100% correlation between holdings). The true portfolio SD requires the covariance matrix of daily returns; treat this as a CONSERVATIVE upper bound. Sharpe is recomputed from the weighted expected return and weighted SD using risk-free 1.5%.
Disclaimer: Portfolio return, risk, Sharpe Ratio and Maximum Drawdown shown above are statistical estimates derived from historical fund performance over the most recent 1-year period. The calculations are provided for illustrative purposes only and do not represent actual portfolio performance. Past performance and historical statistics are not indicative of future results and should not be construed as a guarantee, commitment, or prediction of future returns.
